Look for a step where the problem asks for the total, not just the difference or one part. If you share the exact problem, I can compare your path to the answer key.
This instruction says submit as PDF, but I wrote it in Google Docs.
In Google Docs, use File, Download, PDF Document. Then upload that PDF instead of the doc link. Open the PDF once before submitting to confirm the formatting looks right.
What does use your own words mean on an assignment?
It means explain the idea without copying the source sentence structure. Read the source, close it, write the point from memory, then check that the meaning is still accurate.
I do not understand why my teacher crossed out very unique.
Unique already means one of a kind, so very unique sounds redundant to many teachers. Use unique, unusual, rare, or especially unusual depending on what you mean.
My friend said the movie was fine, but sounded disappointed. What does fine mean there?
In casual speech, fine can mean acceptable but not exciting. If they sounded disappointed, they probably meant the movie was okay, not great.
Can you make this clearer: I might possibly be able to come later?
Say, I may be able to come later. If you want to sound more certain, say, I can come later, but I am not sure what time yet.
What is the difference between borrow and lend?
Borrow means receive something temporarily. Lend means give something temporarily. You borrow a book from Mia. Mia lends the book to you.
Why did my coworker reply noted to my message?
Noted usually means they saw and understood it. It can sound brief, but it is often just a quick acknowledgment, especially in work chat.
Should I reply to that?
Usually no. If they only said noted, the thread is complete unless you need to add a deadline, file, or decision.
Can you explain RSVP in plain language?
RSVP means tell the host whether you are coming. If an invitation says RSVP by Friday, reply before Friday with yes or no.
What does this mean: circle back next week?
It means return to the topic next week. In work settings, it usually means no decision now, but they want to revisit it later.
Someone wrote please advise. Is that annoyed?
Not always. It often just means please tell me what to do next. It can sound formal or slightly sharp, but context matters more than the phrase itself.
How should I answer when someone says thanks for your patience?
You can simply say, No problem, thanks for the update. If it was a customer service chat, Thanks, I appreciate it also works.
Explain the phrase low stakes.
Low stakes means the result is not very serious or risky. Choosing a lunch spot is low stakes. Choosing where to live is higher stakes.
I want to text my neighbor about a package.
Try this: Hi, I think a package for me may have been delivered to your door by mistake. Would you mind checking when you have a chance? Thanks.
How do I reply to a friend who says they are running late?
Say, No worries, thanks for letting me know. I will wait here. If timing matters, add, I can stay until 6:30.
I do not know what to say in a casual birthday message.
Say, Happy birthday, hope you have a great day and get to do something fun. If you know their plans, add one specific line about that.
Can you help me choose a weekend activity?
If you want low effort, choose a walk and coffee. If you want something social, invite one person to lunch. If you want to feel productive, pick one home task and stop after an hour.
I am bored but do not want to leave the house.
Pick a small activity with a clear finish: make popcorn and watch one episode, rearrange one shelf, bake cookies, call someone for 15 minutes, or start a puzzle.
I want something quiet.
Try reading one chapter, sorting photos, doing a puzzle, folding laundry with music, or making tea and journaling for ten minutes.
What should I say when meeting a friend's roommate?
Keep it simple: Hi, I am Alex. Nice to meet you. I have heard a little about you from Jamie. Then ask something easy, like how their week is going.
How do I leave a party politely?
Find the host and say, Thanks for having me. I am going to head out, but it was good to see you. You do not need a long explanation.
I want to be more on time without making a big system.
Set a leave alarm, not just an arrival alarm. Add five minutes for shoes, keys, and locking up. Put the address into maps before you start getting ready.
What is a nice thing to bring when visiting someone?
Bring something easy to use: cookies, fruit, flowers in a small bunch, sparkling water, or coffee beans. Avoid anything that makes the host stop and prepare it right away.
I want to clean my room but it feels like too much.
Start with trash only. Then put dishes in the kitchen. Then clear one surface, like the desk or bed. Stop after 20 minutes if you need to. Visible progress matters first.
How can I make mornings less rushed?
Move two decisions to the night before: clothes and breakfast. Put keys, bag, and charger in one spot. In the morning, do tasks in the same order so you are not deciding while tired.
I do not know what to watch tonight.
Choose based on energy. If you are tired, pick a familiar comedy or cooking show. If you want a story, pick a movie under two hours. Avoid scrolling for more than ten minutes.
What is a simple way to remember names?
Repeat the name once right after hearing it, then connect it to one detail. For example, Maya from the hiking group. Use the name again when you say goodbye.
I like quiet cafes. How do I find one?
Look for photos with small tables, laptop users, and soft lighting. Avoid places described as bustling or great for groups. Go midmorning or midafternoon instead of lunch time.
I prefer simple clothes. How should I shop?
Pick a small color range, then buy pieces that match at least three things you already own. Start with plain shirts, dark jeans or chinos, and one comfortable layer.
I cannot decide between tea and hot chocolate.
Choose tea if you want something lighter and less sweet. Choose hot chocolate if you want a treat. If it is late, pick herbal tea or make a small hot chocolate.
What kind of playlist should I use while cleaning?
Use upbeat songs you already know, not new music that makes you stop and listen closely. A 30-minute playlist also gives you a natural stopping point.
I like gifts that are practical. What should I tell people?
Say, I always like useful things, especially coffee, notebooks, kitchen towels, candles, or snacks. If you want to be specific, share two stores or examples.
I want a hobby that does not require much space.
Try sketching, reading, knitting, language practice, chess puzzles, journaling, origami, or small-batch baking. Choose one with supplies that fit in a drawer.
I am making a tiny website chat demo for a bakery.
Use a warm greeting: Hi, welcome to Sunny Crumb Bakery. I can help with today's menu, pickup times, or custom cake questions. What would you like to know?
Write a website chat reply for a customer asking if a bookstore has a novel in stock.
Thanks for checking. Tell me the title and author, and I can look up availability for pickup today or help you place a hold if it is in stock.
I need sample chat text for a plant shop website.
Hi, welcome to Leaf & Pot. I can help you choose a plant based on light, pet safety, or how often you want to water. What kind of space are you shopping for?
Make it shorter.
Hi, welcome to Leaf & Pot. Need help picking a plant for your space?
Create a friendly chat response for a gym website trial pass question.
Yes, we offer a one-day trial pass. Share your preferred visit date and whether you want a tour, and we will help you get set up at the front desk.
What should a hotel website chat say when rooms are full?
I am sorry, we are fully booked for those dates. I can check nearby dates, add you to a cancellation list, or suggest another room type if your dates are flexible.
Write a chat demo for a pizza shop asking about delivery time.
Delivery is currently estimated at 35 to 45 minutes. If you share your address or ZIP code, I can confirm whether we deliver to your area.
I need a website chat reply for a lost password question.
You can reset your password from the sign-in page. Click Forgot password, enter your email, and check your inbox for the reset link. It may take a minute to arrive.
Make a chat greeting for a local bike repair shop.
Hi, welcome to Ridge Bike Repair. I can help with tune-up options, repair timing, or booking a drop-off. What kind of bike issue are you having?
Write a calm support chat reply for a delayed package.
I can check that for you. Please send the order number and delivery ZIP code, and I will look up the latest tracking update and next expected step.
Create a demo chat message for a coffee subscription website.
Hi, I can help you choose a roast, grind size, and delivery schedule. Do you usually like bright and fruity coffee or smooth and chocolatey coffee?
How do I answer what are you in the mood for?
Name a category and a constraint. For example, I am in the mood for something warm and not too heavy, or I want something crunchy and quick.
I want to make my desk feel nicer without buying much.
Clear the surface, hide loose cables, wipe the keyboard, and add one useful item you already own, like a lamp, mug, or small tray. Remove anything you do not touch daily.
My calendar is full of tiny tasks.
Batch the tiny tasks into one admin block. Keep only time-specific items as separate calendar events. A calendar works better when it shows when you work, not every small click.
I need a quick dinner for one person.
Make a baked potato in the microwave, then top it with cheese, beans, sour cream, salsa, or leftover vegetables. Add a side salad or fruit if you want something fresh.
How can I politely ask someone to lower their music?
Say, Hi, would you mind turning the music down a bit? I am having trouble concentrating. Thanks. Keep it short and ask for the specific change.
I need to pack for one night away.
Pack sleep clothes, one change of clothes, toiletries, charger, medication if you use any, wallet, keys, and a layer. Put tomorrow's outfit together before adding extras.
My oatmeal is boring.
Add one fruit, one crunch, and one flavor. Try banana, walnuts, and cinnamon; berries, granola, and vanilla; or apple, peanut butter, and a pinch of salt.
Can you help me reply to want to grab lunch sometime?
Say, Yes, that would be nice. I am free Tuesday or Thursday next week if either works for you. That shows interest and makes scheduling easier.
I need a simple system for remembering errands.
Keep one running errand note on your phone. Group items by location, like grocery store, pharmacy, post office. Before leaving home, check the note and choose the stops that are near each other.
What is a good quick breakfast if I overslept?
Grab yogurt with granola, peanut butter toast, a banana with nuts, or a cheese sandwich. Choose something you can eat without cooking and take with you if needed.
I do not understand why my recipe says fold, not stir.
Fold means mix gently so you do not knock out air or crush delicate ingredients. Use a spatula, scoop from the bottom, and turn the mixture over slowly until combined.
My backpack is always a mess.
Empty it once a week. Keep only current papers, one pen pouch, one charger, and daily items. Use a folder for loose sheets so they do not sink to the bottom.
I need a quick way to cool down hot coffee.
Pour a little into a wider mug, add a splash of milk, or drop in one coffee ice cube if you have one. Stirring also helps release heat faster.
How do I tell my roommate the sink needs cleaning?
Say, Could you clean the sink today? There is food left in it from last night. I am trying to keep the kitchen usable for both of us. Be specific and calm.
I need to choose a seat for studying at the library.
Choose a seat away from the entrance, printer, and group tables. Sit near an outlet if you need your laptop. If you get distracted, face a wall or window instead of the main room.
What is an LLM?
An LLM is a language model trained to predict text tokens. Chat behavior is added later with formatting and fine-tuning.
Why use BPE instead of characters?
BPE shortens common text chunks, so the model spends fewer steps spelling every word one character at a time.
What does fine-tuning do?
Fine-tuning adjusts a pretrained model toward a narrower behavior, such as answering in a chat format.
What is validation loss?
Validation loss measures prediction error on held-out data. It helps detect whether training progress transfers beyond the training batches.
Why did the model repeat words?
Repetition can come from decoding settings, weak training, biased examples, or the model finding a high-probability loop.
What is weight tying?
Weight tying reuses the token embedding matrix for output logits, saving parameters and linking input and output token meanings.
What is RLHF?
RLHF trains a model using human preferences, often after supervised fine-tuning. For this project, SFT is the simpler next step.
How does attention work?
Attention builds a weighted mix of earlier token vectors so the next-token prediction can use relevant context.
Why increase context window?
A longer context window lets the model condition on more previous tokens, which can help coherence if training data and compute support it.
Should AI take all jobs?
No simple answer. AI changes tasks unevenly; routine digital work is exposed first, while responsibility and trust remain hard to automate.
Explain artificial intelligence example 0.
Artificial intelligence is software doing tasks like classification, prediction, language, or planning. Example 0: a model ranking search results.
How will AI affect jobs case 1?
AI will likely change tasks before whole jobs vanish. Routine writing, coding, and analysis are more exposed than trust-heavy work.
What is machine learning case 2?
Machine learning trains a model from examples so it can generalize patterns to new inputs. The hard part is useful generalization.
Help me with a coding bug case 3.
Find the smallest reproducible input, read the traceback, inspect the changed line, and run one test after each fix.
I am hungry after work case 4.
Make something simple: eggs, pasta, rice and beans, soup, or a sandwich. Add water and something fresh if available.

What is the weather case 7?
I cannot know live weather. Tell me the location and forecast, then I can help decide what it means.
How do I make a hamburger case 8?
Season the patty, cook it safely, toast the bun, then add sauce, vegetables, and cheese if you want.
What should my website demo say case 9?
Say the model is experimental, show streamed output, keep prompts low-stakes, and make limitations visible before users rely on it.
What if the model hallucinates case 10?
Treat hallucination as expected risk. Ask narrower questions, lower temperature, verify facts, and improve training examples.
Give study advice case 11.
Use active recall: answer from memory, check mistakes, then repeat weak parts. Passive rereading feels easier but teaches less.
